## Overview

Base-level activation captures how recently and how often a memory has been used. In ACT-R, base-level activation determines how quickly a memory can be retrieved — recent and frequent uses raise activation, while time without use lets it decay.

Base-level activation is the foundation on which spreading activation operates: spreading adds context-driven boosts on top of the underlying base level. Together they explain why we remember some things faster than others.
